test.name: Initialize an isolated person with no relatives and flag the isolation
test.description: The person_read fixture returns a person with no parents, spouse, or children. The project should still initialize fully, and the summary should flag the isolation as a research constraint (FAN research is harder; reliance on direct records increases). Confirms init does not silently drop or fabricate relatives when the tree person stands alone.

input.user_message: init-project: Start a new research project for Patrick Flynn, FamilySearch ID LZNY-BRF. I want to identify his parents.

judge_context: person-read-flynn returns no parents, spouse, or children — an isolated person. The project must still initialize: both research.json and tree.gedcomx.json created with the Patrick Flynn stub
The summary should flag the isolation — note that with no linked relatives, FAN (Family, Associates, Neighbors) research is harder and reliance on direct records increases
Should NOT fabricate relatives to fill the gap, and should NOT create placeholder unknown-person stubs
tree.gedcomx.json should contain exactly one person (the subject) and no relationships
